pattern: Repeatedly collapsing adjacent pairs mod 10 is the same combining process that generates Pascal's triangle, just reduced modulo 10.
hints: Simulating the collapsing process directly works: at each round, form a new array from every adjacent sum mod 10, and repeat until one element remains.

Notice that the value at a position after one round is nums[i] + nums[i+1], and after two rounds a position depends on three consecutive original values weighted 1, 2, 1 -- the coefficients are binomial.
The final value is the sum of nums[i] times 'n-1 choose i' for every position i, taken mod 10 -- the same coefficients that appear in row n-1 of Pascal's triangle.

edge_inputs.description: two digits collapse in one step
digits sum to a multiple of ten, testing the modulo wraparound
all zero digits stay zero throughout
all nines maximize every intermediate sum
mostly zero digits with a single nonzero digit at one end

stress.description: a full-length array of random digits
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 9) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
